channel.title: The Personal Notes of a Passionate Data Scientist
channel.description: Recent content on The Personal Notes of a Passionate Data Scientist

item.title: Pump It Up – Baseline model
item.description: Baseline model for the Pump-it-Up competition.

item.title: About Me
item.description: About me I am a Lead Data Scientist at Features Analytics, a Belgian Fintech company. I build Machine Learning models and look for anomalies in large datasets. Working Experience February 2017 – Present: Features Analytics, working with R and Python for rapid prototyping, Machine Learning and Data Analysis. November 2011 – January 2017: Johannes Gutentberg Universität, working with R, Python and C++ in Bioinformatics. Also, working as a Tutor, Teaching Assistant and Lecturer.

item.title: How to...
item.description: Print tables from Jupyter This is method to print tables that are properly rendered from Jupyter to Markdown. As a first step, I install the Python package tabulate: pip3 install tabulate From a jupyter cell, if I want to print the data frame df, I use the command line: print(df.head().to_markdown()) The result will be: | ... | ... This will not be rendered as table, but as code (like LaTeX verbatim) due the four trailing spaces.

item.title: Pump It Up – Data Exploration
item.description: Basic Data Exploration for the Pump-it-Up competition.

item.title: Setting up this site
item.description: How this site was set up with hugo and the theme mainroad.
